questions.context:
30 students were asked to choose their favourite type of film.
The results are shown in the table.

| Type of film | Frequency |
|--------------|----------|
| Action       | 9        |
| Comedy       | 6        |
| Drama        | 5        |
| Horror       | 7        |
| Animation    | 3        |

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Work out the angle needed to represent 'Action' in a pie chart.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Work out the angle needed to represent 'Animation' in the pie chart.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Give a reason why a pie chart is suitable for displaying this data.
parts.correctAnswer: It shows proportions of each category out of the whole

markScheme.desc: Shows proportions / parts of a whole / easy to compare categories oe

parts.label: d
parts.questionText:
A student says: 'More than half of the students chose Action or Comedy.'
Is the student correct? Give a reason for your answer.
parts.correctAnswer: Yes — 9+6=15 students which is exactly half, so the statement is incorrect

markScheme.desc: No — 9+6=15 which is exactly half (not more than half) oe

workingSteps: Action + Comedy = 9 + 6 = 15 = 15/30 = exactly half, not more than half

questions.context:
The bar chart shows the number of cars sold by a dealership each month for five months.

| Month    | Cars sold |
|----------|-----------|
| January  | 14        |
| February | 10        |
| March    | 18        |
| April    | 22        |
| May      | 16        |

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Work out the total number of cars sold over the five months.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Work out the mean number of cars sold per month.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText:
In June, the dealership sold 25 cars.
Describe how this would affect the mean.
parts.correctAnswer: The mean would increase because 25 is greater than the current mean of 16

markScheme.desc: Mean increases from 16 to 17.5 with correct working

parts.label: d
parts.questionText: Give a reason why a bar chart is appropriate for displaying monthly car sales.
parts.correctAnswer: A bar chart clearly shows and compares discrete data across different categories (months)

markScheme.desc: Shows discrete data / easy to compare values across different months oe

parts.label: e
parts.questionText: Work out the range of the monthly car sales.

questions.context:
A frequency polygon is drawn showing the heights (cm) of 40 plants.
The points are plotted at midpoints 15, 25, 35, 45, 55 with frequencies 4, 9, 14, 10, 3.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Work out the total number of plants.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Work out an estimate for the mean height.

parts.label: c
workingSteps: Highest frequency = 14 at midpoint 35, so modal class is 30 < h ≤ 40
